title: Slow-cooker butter chicken
subtitle: Murgh makhani in the slow cooker
excerpt: Chicken thighs simmered all day in a spiced tomato sauce with butter, cream, and garam masala until the sauce is rich and deeply flavoured and the meat is falling apart. The best slow-cooker curry. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Murgh makhani (butter chicken) was created in Delhi in the 1950s, attributed to Kundan Lal Gujral of Moti Mahal restaurant, who simmered leftover tandoori chicken in a tomato and butter sauce to prevent it from drying out. The dish became the most ordered Indian curry in Britain. The slow-cooker version cannot replicate the tandoor char on the chicken but produces the sauce well. The recipe draws on versions by Meera Sodha in Fresh India (2016) and Monisha Bharadwaj in The Indian Cookery Course (2012).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the sauce and chicken together for up to two months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the day before and refrigerate. Reheat gently and stir in the cream just before serving.

recipeTools.notes: For a smooth sauce. Leave unblended for a chunkier result.

content.text: The butter and cream go in at different points. The cream stirs in at the end, just before serving, to stay fresh and prevent it splitting over the long cook. The butter goes in at the start, where it enriches the sauce and contributes the characteristic makhani flavour. Do not omit either.

Add the {{tinned-tomatoes}} of tomatoes, {{onion}} of finely chopped onion, {{garlic}} of grated garlic, {{ginger-root}} of grated ginger, {{garam-masala}} of garam masala, {{cumin-ground}} of ground cumin, {{paprika-sweet}} of sweet paprika, {{unsalted-butter}} of butter, and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt to the slow cooker. Stir to combine. Add the chicken thigh pieces and turn to coat. Cover and cook on LOW for 5–6 hours or HIGH for 3–4 hours until the chicken is tender and cooked through. Stir in {{double-cream}} of double cream. If you prefer a smooth sauce, use a stick blender before adding the cream. Taste for seasoning and serve with basmati rice and naan.

items.symptom: Sauce splitting — fat separated from the tomato base
items.cause: Cream added too early and cooked for too long.
items.fix: Always add the cream in the last 10 minutes before serving. Blending the sauce before adding the cream also helps emulsification.

items.cause: Breast used instead of thighs, or overcooked.
items.fix: Boneless thighs only. Breast dries out in a slow cooker after 3 hours. Check at 5 hours on LOW and pull as soon as the chicken shreds easily.

items.cause: Slow cooking mellows spices over time.
items.fix: Stir in another half teaspoon of garam masala with the cream at the end. Adjust heat to taste with a pinch of chilli flakes.

content.text: Murgh makhani as made at Moti Mahal in Delhi used leftover tandoori chicken, so the meat already had char and spice from the tandoor before the butter sauce touched it. The slow-cooker version has none of that char, but it has something the tandoor version cannot offer: the chicken cooks inside the sauce rather than being added to it, which means every fibre carries the flavour of the tomato and spice all the way through.
